pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh shows exactly 10 test functions are generated across 3 test files (4 in test_parser.py, 3 in test_stats.py, 3 in test_anomaly.py). verifier.py also hardcodes checks for 4, 3, and 3 tests respectively.
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value to determine the final pass status.
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value for the parser component score.
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value for the stats component score.
Confirmed via verifier.py which uses this value for the anomaly component score.
Confirmed via verifier.py which explicitly hardcodes a 5-point bonus for a clean run without remaining stubs.

privileged_info_summary: The target project is located at /home/ga/PycharmProjects/log_analyzer. While the task description and metadata claim there are 17 required tests, the environment setup actually only generates 10 tests (4 parser tests, 3 stats tests, and 3 anomaly tests). The scoring system allocates 35 points for parser implementation, 40 points for stats, 20 points for anomaly detection, and a 5-point clean run bonus, with a total passing threshold of 65 points. The sample access.log data used for testing is entirely synthetic and dynamically generated during task setup.
